Code Examples

Practical examples for common scenarios

â†?Back to Index

Data Import Examples

Import Data from Database
Load data from a database and write it to a worksheet.
using (var workbook = new Workbook())
{
    var worksheet = workbook.Worksheets[0];
    worksheet.Name = "Data";

    // Simulating database data
    var data = new[]
    {
        new { Name = "Alice", Age = 30, City = "New York" },
        new { Name = "Bob", Age = 25, City = "Los Angeles" },
        new { Name = "Charlie", Age = 35, City = "Chicago" }
    };

    // Write headers
    worksheet.Cells["A1"].PutValue("Name");
    worksheet.Cells["B1"].PutValue("Age");
    worksheet.Cells["C1"].PutValue("City");

    // Write data
    for (int i = 0; i < data.Length; i++)
    {
        worksheet.Cells[i + 2, 0].PutValue(data[i].Name);
        worksheet.Cells[i + 2, 1].PutValue(data[i].Age);
        worksheet.Cells[i + 2, 2].PutValue(data[i].City);
    }

    workbook.Save("DatabaseImport.xlsx");
}
Import Data from CSV
Read data from a CSV file and import into a worksheet.
using (var workbook = new Workbook())
{
    var worksheet = workbook.Worksheets[0];
    worksheet.Name = "CSV Data";

    // Read CSV file
    var lines = File.ReadAllLines("data.csv");
    for (int row = 0; row < lines.Length; row++)
    {
        var columns = lines[row].Split(',');
        for (int col = 0; col < columns.Length; col++)
        {
            worksheet.Cells[row, col].PutValue(columns[col].Trim());
        }
    }

    workbook.Save("CSVImport.xlsx");
}

Formatting Examples

Create a Styled Report
Apply comprehensive formatting to create a professional-looking report.
using (var workbook = new Workbook())
{
    var worksheet = workbook.Worksheets[0];
    worksheet.Name = "Quarterly Report";

    // Create title
    worksheet.Cells["A1"].PutValue("Quarterly Sales Report");
    var titleStyle = worksheet.Cells["A1"].GetStyle();
    titleStyle.Font.Size = 16;
    titleStyle.Font.Bold = true;
    titleStyle.Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.White;
    titleStyle.Pattern = FillPattern.Solid;
    titleStyle.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.DarkBlue;
    titleStyle.HorizontalAlignment = HorizontalAlignmentType.Center;
    worksheet.Cells["A1"].SetStyle(titleStyle);
    worksheet.Cells.Merge(0, 0, 1, 4);

    // Create table headers
    string[] headers = { "Product", "Q1", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4", "Total" };
    for (int i = 0; i < headers.Length; i++)
    {
        worksheet.Cells[2, i].PutValue(headers[i]);
        var headerStyle = worksheet.Cells[2, i].GetStyle();
        headerStyle.Font.Bold = true;
        headerStyle.Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.White;
        headerStyle.Pattern = FillPattern.Solid;
        headerStyle.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.Gray;
        worksheet.Cells[2, i].SetStyle(headerStyle);
    }

    // Add sample data
    object[,] data = {
        { "Product A", 10000, 12000, 15000, 18000 },
        { "Product B", 8000, 9500, 11000, 12500 },
        { "Product C", 12000, 14000, 16000, 19000 }
    };

    for (int row = 0; row < data.GetLength(0); row++)
    {
        for (int col = 0; col < data.GetLength(1); col++)
        {
            worksheet.Cells[row + 3, col].PutValue(data[row, col]);
        }
    }

    // Add total formulas
    for (int row = 0; row < 3; row++)
    {
        string formula = $"=SUM(B{row + 4}:E{row + 4})";
        worksheet.Cells[row + 3, 5].Formula = formula;
    }

    // Format numbers
    var numberStyle = worksheet.Cells["B4"].GetStyle();
    numberStyle.NumberFormat = "$#,##0";
    for (int row = 3; row < 6; row++)
    {
        for (int col = 1; col < 6; col++)
        {
            worksheet.Cells[row, col].SetStyle(numberStyle);
        }
    }

    // Auto-fit columns
    worksheet.Cells.Columns[0].AutoFit();
    worksheet.Cells.Columns[1].AutoFit();
    worksheet.Cells.Columns[2].AutoFit();
    worksheet.Cells.Columns[3].AutoFit();
    worksheet.Cells.Columns[4].AutoFit();
    worksheet.Cells.Columns[5].AutoFit();

    workbook.Save("QuarterlyReport.xlsx");
}
Conditional Formatting
Apply conditional formatting to highlight specific values.
using (var workbook = new Workbook())
{
    var worksheet = workbook.Worksheets[0];

    // Add sample data
    worksheet.Cells["A1"].PutValue("Score");
    for (int i = 0; i < 10; i++)
    {
        worksheet.Cells[i + 1, 0].PutValue(new Random().Next(0, 100));
    }

    // Add conditional formatting for values > 75
    var condition = worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add();
    var formatCondition = condition.Add(FormatConditionType.CellValue,
        OperatorType.Greater, "75", "");

    // Set style for high values
    var style = formatCondition.Style;
    style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.LightGreen;
    style.Pattern = FillPattern.Solid;
    formatCondition.Style = style;

    workbook.Save("ConditionalFormatting.xlsx");
}

Chart Examples

Basic Chart Creation
Create a simple chart from worksheet data.
using (var workbook = new Workbook())
{
    var worksheet = workbook.Worksheets[0];
    worksheet.Name = "Sales Data";

    // Add data
    worksheet.Cells["A1"].PutValue("Month");
    worksheet.Cells["B1"].PutValue("Sales");

    string[] months = { "Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun" };
    int[] sales = { 45000, 52000, 48000, 61000, 55000, 67000 };

    for (int i = 0; i < months.Length; i++)
    {
        worksheet.Cells[i + 1, 0].PutValue(months[i]);
        worksheet.Cells[i + 1, 1].PutValue(sales[i]);
    }

    // Create a chart
    int chartIndex = worksheet.Charts.Add(ChartType.Column, 5, 0, 15, 5);
    var chart = worksheet.Charts[chartIndex];
    chart.Title.Text = "Monthly Sales";
    chart.PlotArea.Area.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.White;

    // Set data source
    chart.NSeries.Add("B2:B7", false);
    chart.NSeries.CategoryData = "A2:A7";

    workbook.Save("SalesChart.xlsx");
}

Advanced Examples

Multiple Worksheets with Cross-Sheet References
Create a workbook with multiple worksheets and formulas that reference other sheets.
using (var workbook = new Workbook())
{
    // Create first worksheet - Data
    var dataSheet = workbook.Worksheets[0];
    dataSheet.Name = "Data";

    // Add data
    worksheet.Cells["A1"].PutValue("Product");
    worksheet.Cells["B1"].PutValue("Price");
    worksheet.Cells["A2"].PutValue("Apple");
    worksheet.Cells["B2"].PutValue(1.99);
    worksheet.Cells["A3"].PutValue("Banana");
    worksheet.Cells["B3"].PutValue(0.99);

    // Create second worksheet - Summary
    int summarySheetIndex = workbook.Worksheets.Add("Summary");`r`n    var summarySheet = workbook.Worksheets[summarySheetIndex];

    // Add formula referencing data sheet
    summarySheet.Cells["A1"].PutValue("Total Value");
    summarySheet.Cells["A2"].Formula = "=SUM(Data!B2:B3)";

    workbook.Save("MultipleSheets.xlsx");
}
Data Validation
Add data validation to restrict user input.
using (var workbook = new Workbook())
{
    var worksheet = workbook.Worksheets[0];

    // Create a dropdown list
    worksheet.Cells["A1"].PutValue("Category");
    worksheet.Cells["A2"].PutValue("Fruit");

    // Add data validation
    var validation = worksheet.Validations.Add();
    validation.Type = ValidationType.List;
    validation.Operator = OperatorType.Between;
    validation.InCellDropDown = true;
    validation.Formula1 = "Fruit,Vegetable,Dairy,Meat";

    // Apply validation to cell
    var area = new CellArea();
    area.StartRow = 2;
    area.StartColumn = 0;
    area.EndRow = 10;
    area.EndColumn = 0;
    validation.AddArea(area);

    workbook.Save("DataValidation.xlsx");
}
Page Setup for Printing
Configure page setup settings for professional printing.
using (var workbook = new Workbook())
{
    var worksheet = workbook.Worksheets[0];
    worksheet.Name = "Print Layout";

    // Add some data
    for (int row = 0; row < 50; row++)
    {
        for (int col = 0; col < 10; col++)
        {
            worksheet.Cells[row, col].PutValue($"Cell {row},{col}");
        }
    }

    // Configure page setup
    var pageSetup = worksheet.PageSetup;
    pageSetup.Orientation = PageOrientationType.Landscape;
    pageSetup.PaperSize = PaperSizeType.PaperA4;
    pageSetup.LeftMargin = 0.5;
    pageSetup.RightMargin = 0.5;
    pageSetup.TopMargin = 0.75;
    pageSetup.BottomMargin = 0.75;

    // Add headers and footers
    pageSetup.SetHeader(0, "&CCompany Name");
    pageSetup.SetHeader(2, "&D&T");
    pageSetup.SetFooter(0, "&CPage &P of &N");

    // Set print area
    pageSetup.PrintArea = "A1:J50";

    workbook.Save("PrintLayout.xlsx");
}

Tips and Best Practices

Performance Tips:
  • Use using statement to ensure proper disposal of Workbook objects
  • Batch cell operations when possible to reduce overhead
  • Reuse Style objects instead of creating new ones for similar formatting
  • Consider using cell ranges for bulk operations
Memory Management:
  • Always dispose Workbook objects when done to free resources
  • For large datasets, process data in chunks
  • Clear unused worksheets to reduce memory usage
